{"step_by_step_thinking": "Radical neck dissection involves the removal of a wide range of structures in the neck, including lymph nodes, nerves, and other tissues. The spinal accessory nerve (A), submandibular gland (B), and tail of the parotid gland (C) are all structures that can be involved in this procedure. However, the tail of the parotid gland is not typically removed during a radical neck dissection, as it is not part of the central neck compartment where the majority of lymph nodes are located. Level 2b lymph nodes (D) are part of the central neck compartment and are usually removed during a radical neck dissection.", "answer_choice": "C"}
